premise: I spent two decades collecting our grandmother’s handwritten recipes into a cookbook, trusting my TV-chef brother to help publish it, but he filed copyright in his own name, landed a $380,000 deal, and opened a chain of ‘secret family recipe’ restaurants while branding me a liar. After years of litigation I reclaimed the copyright, won every royalty plus a judgment that forced him to sell the flagship location—I bought it at auction, renamed it ‘The True Matriarch,’ and now serve each dish with a card bearing our grandmother’s story and the court docket number of his fraud.

thumb.label: Thumbnail + metadata
thumb.detail: My Brother Stole My Grandmother's Recipes And Got $380K. So I Bought His Restaur
